title: Member Joining Form with Admin Dashboard Integration and Unique Member ID

requirements.summary: Create a Member Joining Form that collects registration details and displays the newly generated unique member ID (RI format) upon successful submission
acceptanceCriteria: The joining form displays input fields for name, mobile, email, and optional sponsor ID.
Validation errors are shown for missing or invalid required fields.
On successful registration, the new unique member ID is displayed prominently to the user.
The form is accessible via the app navigation.

file_operations.description: Update the RegisterPage to display the newly generated unique member ID (from MemberRegistrationResult.memberIdStr) in the RI format prominently after successful registration. Show the ID in a success card or modal with clear formatting. Ensure the form validates all required fields (name, mobile, email) before submission and displays validation errors inline. The form already collects name, mobile, email, and optional sponsor ID per the existing structure.

Verify the useRegisterMember mutation returns MemberRegistrationResult containing both id and memberIdStr fields. Ensure the mutation properly exposes the memberIdStr field (the RI-formatted ID) to consuming components. Update type definitions if necessary to reflect the backend interface signature.

requirements.summary: Ensure newly registered members appear immediately in the Admin Dashboard member list with their unique RI-format member ID displayed as a column
acceptanceCriteria: A newly registered member appears in the admin panel member list upon their next load or after triggering a refresh.
The admin member table includes a column showing each member's unique RI-format ID.
The admin panel React Query cache is invalidated after a new member is registered so the list reflects the latest data.

file_operations.description: Add a new column to the admin member table displaying the memberIdStr field (the RI-formatted unique member ID) for each member. Position this column early in the table (e.g., after the row number or as the first data column) so it is prominently visible. Update the table headers and cell rendering logic to include this new column. Ensure the column is searchable/sortable if those features exist.

After successful member registration via useRegisterMember mutation, invalidate the React Query cache for the 'members' query key (used by AdminPanel to list all members) so that the admin panel reflects the newly registered member immediately upon next load. Use queryClient.invalidateQueries(['members']) in the onSuccess callback of the mutation.

Ensure the 'Register Member' navigation link is visible and accessible to users with appropriate roles (admin or authenticated users). Verify the link correctly routes to the RegisterPage so users can access the joining form via app navigation.
